answer:
<think> The Catilinarian conspiracy was a plot to overthrow the Roman Republic in the 1st century BC. One of the key figures involved was Catiline, a Roman senator. A general who played a critical role in the transformation of the Roman Republic was Julius Caesar. I believe there is a connection between Catiline and Caesar, as Caesar was a contemporary of Catiline and was known to have been involved in the politics of the time. I recall that Catiline was a rival of Cicero, who was a prominent Roman statesman and orator, and that Cicero had a famous speech against Catiline. I also remember that Caesar was a close ally of Cicero and that they shared a common enemy in Catiline. Furthermore, I know that Caesar's rise to power was a key factor in the transformation of the Roman Republic, and that his eventual assassination was a turning point in Roman history. I believe that Catiline and Caesar were connected through their shared involvement in the politics of the late Roman Republic, and that their rivalry was a significant factor in the events that led to Caesar's rise to power.</think>
<answer> Cicero </answer>
